Serving All of Comal County, TX

Comal County Stucco Repair & Installation

Comal County blends Hill Country beauty with steady residential growth, and keeping your home's exterior in top shape is essential in this climate. Star Stucco of Austin has spent over a decade serving homeowners across New Braunfels, Canyon Lake, Bulverde, and the surrounding communities with stucco repair, installation, and moisture protection services built to last.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Protecting Comal County Homes from the Elements

Comal County occupies a prime stretch of the Texas Hill Country where limestone bluffs, spring-fed rivers, and wooded hillsides create stunning settings for residential properties. But the same geography that makes the area beautiful also subjects homes to driving rain, flash flooding, and intense UV radiation that break down exterior finishes over time.

The county experiences some of the highest rainfall totals in the Hill Country, with sudden downpours that can dump several inches in a matter of hours. Stucco walls that lack adequate moisture barriers or weep systems absorb this water and develop soft spots, mold, and structural decay behind the finish. Moisture management is not optional here — it is the foundation of any lasting stucco job.

From established neighborhoods in New Braunfels to newer developments around Bulverde and Spring Branch, we have seen every type of stucco failure that Comal County weather can produce. Our approach prioritizes proper drainage details, quality materials, and finishes that look great while standing up to the demands of this environment.

Stucco Services We Offer in Comal County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Comal County.

Stucco Repair

Comal County stucco takes a beating from heavy rains and scorching summers. We repair cracked, chipped, and water-damaged stucco using materials matched to your existing finish, restoring both appearance and weatherproofing in a single visit when possible.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

New homes across Bulverde, Spring Branch, and the Canyon Lake corridor benefit from stucco exteriors that combine curb appeal with low maintenance. We install three-coat and one-coat systems with integrated moisture management designed for Comal County rainfall.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

Limestone karst terrain in Comal County creates localized ground movement that leads to stucco cracking around corners, windows, and control joints. We route and fill cracks with elastomeric compounds that flex with future movement rather than splitting open again.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Water damage is the number one stucco issue in Comal County. Flash floods, sustained rain, and poor original drainage details allow moisture behind walls where it quietly destroys sheathing and framing. We open affected areas, dry and treat the structure, and rebuild with proper barriers.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Historic properties in New Braunfels and the Gruene area feature plaster exteriors that reflect the region's German heritage. We restore these finishes with traditional lime plaster techniques and period-appropriate textures that honor the original craftsmanship.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

Synthetic stucco systems on Comal County homes need drainage cavities and proper termination details to survive the area's intense rain events. We install EIFS with full back-drainage and repair existing systems that trap moisture due to original design shortcomings.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Businesses along the I-35 corridor in New Braunfels and Garden Ridge count on appealing stucco facades to draw customers. We provide commercial stucco installation and repair services on schedules that minimize downtime for your operation.

Learn More

Why Comal County Property Owners Rely on Star Stucco

Moisture management expertise developed through hundreds of projects in one of the wettest Hill Country counties

Familiarity with local building styles from historic German-Texas architecture to modern Hill Country designs

Thorough inspections using moisture meters and infrared tools to find hidden water damage before it spreads

Premium materials rated for high-moisture environments, not bargain products that fail within a few years

Crews trained specifically in drainage-plane installation, kick-out flashings, and weep screed detailing

Straightforward communication — we explain every finding and recommendation without pressure or upselling

Local Expertise

We understand Comal County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Comal County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Comal County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Comal County

Heavy rainfall, limestone terrain, and intense heat create a challenging environment for stucco in Comal County. These are the problems we encounter and resolve most frequently.

Cracks

Karst geology beneath much of Comal County causes uneven settling that translates into stucco cracks. Diagonal cracks near window corners and horizontal cracks along floor lines are especially common in areas with shallow limestone bedrock.

Bubbling or soft stucco

Prolonged exposure to rain-saturated conditions causes the scratch and brown coats to absorb water and lose adhesion. Bubbling often appears on lower wall sections where splash-back from landscaping keeps the stucco wet for extended periods.

Water stains

Mineral-laden groundwater and limestone dust create chalky white staining on stucco walls after rain. These efflorescence deposits indicate moisture is moving through the wall system and should be investigated promptly.

Window and door leaks

Many Comal County homes lack proper head flashings above windows, relying solely on caulk that eventually fails. When that seal breaks, rain runs down the rough opening and into the wall, causing damage that is invisible until it reaches interior finishes.

Failing patches

Patch repairs that were applied without cutting back to sound material or using proper bonding agents are a recurring issue on Comal County homes. These superficial fixes trap moisture underneath and accelerate deterioration of the surrounding stucco.

Separation around trim

Composite and PVC trim on newer homes in Comal County expand at different rates than the surrounding stucco, opening gaps that collect wind-driven rain. Proper backer rod and sealant joints are needed to maintain a watertight perimeter.

Impact damage

Severe thunderstorms bring large hail to Comal County with regularity, leaving dents and fractures in stucco surfaces. Tree limbs and construction debris during storm events add to the damage on exposed walls.

Faded or stained exterior walls

Constant sun exposure fades stucco pigment on south- and west-facing walls, while shaded areas develop dark mildew stains fed by Comal County humidity. A combination of cleaning and color-matched refinishing restores a uniform appearance.

How We Work in Comal County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule inspection

Give us a call or submit a request online. We serve all of Comal County from New Braunfels to the Canyon Lake communities and can usually book your inspection within a few days.

2

Review scope

Our specialist walks your property, identifies every area of concern, and uses moisture detection equipment to reveal damage that is not visible on the surface. We take the time to explain what we find and what it means for your home.

3

Get estimate

You receive a comprehensive written estimate that covers materials, labor, and projected timeline. We welcome questions and will revise the scope with you until it fits your priorities and budget.

4

Complete work

Our team executes the project on the agreed schedule, maintaining a safe and organized work area throughout. We conduct a final walkthrough together and make sure everything meets your expectations before the job is closed.
